Biomechanics: study of forces acting on and generated within body and their effect on tissue, fluid
## Property of Biological Tissue

Tissues exhibit **viscoelasticity**
Can exhibit both ==viscous(Thick fluid)== and ==elastic(rubber band like)== characteristics during deformation ^e88993

Allow ==stretch/compress and return to original shape when force removed==

Therefore can follow a variation of **Elastic modulus**

That has 3 main phases:

- Elasticity phase: stretch, can ==return to original shape==
- Strain hardening phase(plasticity): experiencing strain high enough ==might not return to original shape== ^90d32d
- Necking(failure) phase: close to ==failure, material might break/fail==
Similar to [[elasticity model]] in physics
Application for biological tissue:

- Toe region: little inconsistent stretching, collagen starting to uncoil

- ![](data:image/png;base64,)

- Elastic region: fibres are unfurled, can recoil
- Plastic region: ==long term lengthening of fibres==, might be good, might be bad ==depending on force application==

Too much force -> low grade injury
- Failure: high grade injury/rupture
![[Pasted image 20250126000934.png]]
![[Pasted image 20250126000938.png]]

## Types of forces affecting tissue behaviour

Different types of forces to apply on tissue , [[Time & Rate dependent factors]] affect the result

Types of forces:

- Tension - stretching material
- Compression - pressing into material
- Bending - pushing ends of material in same direction
- Shear - opposite direction forces exerted on distal parts
- Torsion - twisting about 1 point, while other is held or twisted in oppo direction
- Combined loading - 2 of more types of forces acting simultaneously
